fail_to_pass.name: the_dynamic_resolver_is_consulted_on_every_account-linking_e
fail_to_pass.description:
The dynamic resolver is consulted on every account-linking entry point,
not just OAuth callback. Story exercises the same dynamic-trust
contract through `client.signIn.social` + `/callback/google` interplay
(same public surface, but driven through the typed test client rather
than raw auth.handler — proves the feature is wired through both the
routing plumbing and the lower-level handler).

fail_to_pass.name: the_dynamic_resolver's_return_value_drives_the_trust_decisio
fail_to_pass.description:
The dynamic resolver's return value drives the trust decision: when
the returned list does NOT contain the OAuth provider, an
unverified-email account-linking attempt for that provider is rejected
— confirming the implementation evaluates the resolver's content
rather than merely accepting the function shape.

fail_to_pass.name: the_dynamic_resolver_receives_the_incoming_request_and_can_p
fail_to_pass.description:
The dynamic resolver receives the incoming Request and can produce
per-request-varying trust outcomes. With a single resolver that
derives its return from properties of the request (header, query
parameter, URL path), two callbacks made with different request
inputs produce different outcomes — confirming per-request invocation
rather than a once-cached value.

fail_to_pass.name: a_function-shaped_trustedproviders_configuration_is_accepted
fail_to_pass.description:
A function-shaped trustedProviders configuration is accepted by the
public BetterAuthOptions surface. This documents the API-contract
change: the option's static type widened from T[] to T[] | ((request?:
Request) => Awaitable<T[]>).

fail_to_pass.name:
the_public_betterauthoptions_documentation_explains_that
tru
fail_to_pass.description:
The public BetterAuthOptions documentation explains that
trustedProviders mirrors trustedOrigins' static-or-async-function
shape, so application authors know they can supply a per-request
resolver.

fail_to_pass.name: every_existing_account-linking_trust_check_has_been_updated
fail_to_pass.description:
Every existing account-linking trust check has been updated to use the
resolved trusted-providers list (whether stored on AuthContext or
resolved inline at the callsite). This must include the interactive
linkSocial endpoint, the OAuth callback handler, the lower-level
handleOAuthUserInfo helper, the Google One Tap plugin's existing-user
branch, and the SAML SSO callback + ACS endpoints.

pass_to_pass.name:
static-array_trustedproviders_still_works._configuring
`trus
pass_to_pass.description:
Static-array trustedProviders still works. Configuring
`trustedProviders: ['google']` (the pre-extension shape) keeps the same
unverified-email-linking behavior — regression guard.
